channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Mayon Volcano Eruption Causes Widespread Ashfall Across Albay Communities
item.description: Residents in various towns and villages across Albay are experiencing heavy ashfall following recent activity from the Mayon Volcano. The ashfall has blanketed streets, homes, and farmlands, prompting...

item.title: Investigators Probe Circumstances of Dr Obwaka’s Death After Visit to Woman’s Home in Kitengela
item.description: Detectives in Kenya are investigating the circumstances surrounding the death of 83-year-old Dr Job Obwaka. On the evening of May 1, Dr Obwaka collapsed in Kitengela and was rushed to The Nairobi Hosp...

item.title: US and Philippine Forces Test Anti-Ship Missile Deployment in Batanes During Joint Drills
item.description: Nagpakita ang puwersa ng Estados Unidos at Pilipinas ng Navy-Marine Expeditionary Ship Interdiction System (NMESIS) sa Batanes bilang bahagi ng taunang Balikatan war games, sa gitna ng patuloy na tens...

item.title: Debate over potential restrictions on pro-Palestine demonstrations in the UK
item.description: Organisers of pro-Palestine marches have voiced strong opposition to Prime Minister Keir Starmer's suggestion that some demonstrations opposing Israel could be banned. Starmer, speaking on BBC Radio 4...

item.title: 2027 Ogun East Senatorial Contest: Abiodun vs Daniel
item.description: The 2027 senatorial election for Ogun East is shaping up to be a major political showdown as two former allies, Governor Dapo Abiodun and ex-Governor Gbenga Daniel, prepare to contest. Senator Daniel ...

item.title: Liberal Party wins Nepean by-election, boosting momentum before Victorian state election
item.description: The Liberal Party has secured a significant victory in the Victorian by-election for the seat of Nepean, a result widely seen as an early indicator of voter sentiment ahead of the state election sched...

item.title: Global Esports defeats defending champions Nongshim RedForce in VCT Pacific Stage 1
item.description: In an exciting turn of events at the 2026 VCT Pacific: Stage 1, Global Esports emerged victorious against the defending champions, Nongshim RedForce. This remarkable win highlights the rising competit...

item.title: Authorities Lay Hate Crime Charges in Toronto Event Disruption
item.description: Toronto police have announced new hate crime charges against individuals involved in the disruption of a pro-Israel event in the city. The charges follow investigations into incidents where rioters in...

item.title: FIFA Confirms Iran Will Participate in 2026 World Cup in the US
item.description: FIFA President Gianni Infantino has officially confirmed that Iran will take part in the 2026 FIFA World Cup in the United States, despite recent geopolitical tensions. The announcement came during th...

item.title: Court Denies Pre-Arrest Bail to Nida Khan in Nashik TCS Case
item.description: A court has rejected anticipatory bail for Nida Khan, who is a key accused in a case involving alleged sexual exploitation and religious coercion at a Tata Consultancy Services (TCS) BPO in Nashik. Th...

item.title: Canada’s flag football team prepares for world championship with Olympic qualification in sight
item.description: Canada’s men’s flag football team is holding a selection camp in Winnipeg as it prepares for the 2026 International Federation of American Football (IFAF) world championship in Düsseldorf, Germany. Th...

item.title: Canadian duo Kadriana and Colton Lott secure bronze at world mixed doubles curling championship
item.description: Canada's Kadriana and Colton Lott captured the bronze medal at the World Mixed Doubles Curling Championship with a commanding 11-3 victory over Italy’s Stefania Constantini and Amos Mosaner in Geneva....

item.title: NATO Requests Clarification on U.S. Troop Reduction in Germany Amid Iran Tensions
item.description: The United States has ordered the withdrawal of approximately 5,000 troops from Germany over the next year, implementing President Donald Trump's earlier threats to reduce American military presence i...

item.title: McLaughlin-Phillips boosts Wallabies hopes as Reds defeat Brumbies 30-21
item.description: Queensland Reds playmaker Harry McLaughlin-Phillips has strengthened his case for Wallabies selection after a composed and influential performance in a 30-21 win over the Brumbies. Stepping into the s...

item.title: Commonwealth Avenue closed this weekend for light rail works
item.description: Commonwealth Avenue in Canberra is fully closed in both directions this weekend between Parkes Way and Vernon Circle as part of the ongoing light rail extension from the city to Commonwealth Park. The...

item.title: Tamil Nadu Political Landscape Likely to Change Following Assembly Election Outcome
item.description: The recent assembly election verdict in Tamil Nadu indicates a significant shift in the state's political dynamics. With the traditional Dravidian parties, DMK and AIADMK, competing closely over decad...

item.title: Rajnath Singh Hails Operation Sindoor as Key Anti-Terrorism Milestone
item.description: Defence Minister Rajnath Singh praised Operation Sindoor as a true reflection of the Modi government's unwavering resolve to wipe out terrorism through bold and decisive actions. Speaking at the 'Shau...

item.title: Cuba Faces Growing Economic Pressure as U.S. Expands Sanctions
item.description: The Trump administration has significantly escalated sanctions against Cuba, targeting international companies operating on the island. New economic measures now allow the U.S. to penalize foreign ent...

item.title: Bryan Johnson Shares Insights on Girlfriend’s Vaginal Health and Microbiome
item.description: Bryan Johnson, a well-known biohacker, recently shared detailed information about the vaginal health of his girlfriend, Kate Tolo, on social media. Johnson highlighted that Tolo's vaginal microbiome i...

item.title: NIA Requests Extension of Angat Dam Irrigation Water for Remaining Rice Farms in Bulacan and Pampanga
item.description: Operators of the Angat Dam in Bulacan have been requested by the National Irrigation Administration (NIA) to extend the release of irrigation water for rice farms in Central Luzon. The request came af...
